jokes.text: Oh, my hair!
jokes.setup: Bizarre atmospheric conditions affecting the city
jokes.punchline: Character's concern is purely vanity-based amid chaos
jokes.explanation: Simple vanity joke amid catastrophe - functional but predictable

jokes.text: Lot of weather we're having. Allow me, my lady.
jokes.setup: Extreme weather conditions destroying the city
jokes.punchline: Fry's massive understatement followed by chivalrous formality
jokes.explanation: Classic understatement humor with character-appropriate gallantry

jokes.text: Shall we go a-trousering?
jokes.setup: Fry continuing his overly formal romantic gestures
jokes.punchline: Made-up archaic verb for retrieving pants
jokes.explanation: Inventive wordplay that perfectly captures Fry's romantic delusions

jokes.text: You're right. I'm sorry for showing my love.
jokes.setup: Leela calling out Fry's excessive romantic gestures
jokes.punchline: Fry's melodramatic self-pity response
jokes.explanation: Solid character moment showing Fry's emotional immaturity

jokes.text: My pants! My lucky pants! They don't look so lucky to me. They are, too! I was wearing them that time I found a dime in my ear. I was wearing them when I won a subscription to Redbook. And I was wearing them when I first met Leela, so yeah, they're lucky.
jokes.setup: Fry's pants being blown away by weather, claim they're lucky
jokes.punchline: Escalating list of increasingly mundane 'lucky' events culminating in meeting Leela
jokes.explanation: Well-structured escalation with character-appropriate delusions and sweet payoff

jokes.text: Plus, they're my only pants. You've worn the same pants for 1,000 years? No wonder they made a run for it.
jokes.setup: Fry admitting the pants are his only pair
jokes.punchline: Anthropomorphizing the pants as escaping their disgusting fate
jokes.explanation: Strong visual comedy with perfect setup-punchline structure

jokes.text: Soon your trousers will slip the surly bonds of Earth and ascend to the heavens. I'm gonna blast that balloon! No way will I let God get my pants! Nobody messes with my pants! Not even the Holy One, blessed be He.
jokes.setup: Professor's poetic description of pants ascending to heaven
jokes.punchline: Fry's increasingly aggressive threats against God over pants
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation from poetic to blasphemous, perfectly captures Fry's priorities

jokes.text: Look out! A Central Park badger!
jokes.setup: Characters in underground tunnels
jokes.punchline: Random dangerous wildlife in urban setting
jokes.explanation: Simple random animal humor, functional but not particularly clever

jokes.text: Scruffy, do you have any varmint grease? What viscosity you need?
jokes.setup: Need for grease to deal with badger
jokes.punchline: Scruffy matter-of-factly asking about technical specifications for animal grease
jokes.explanation: Perfect Scruffy moment - treating absurd situation with professional competence

jokes.text: Also, this grease is flammable.
jokes.setup: Already established dangerous situation with grease
jokes.punchline: Casual afterthought about additional danger
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan delivery of escalating danger as casual information

jokes.text: No, this isn't mutant language. We use a lot more profanity. Son of a...
jokes.setup: Trying to identify mysterious writing, suggesting it might be mutant language
jokes.punchline: Mutant immediately proving point by starting to swear then cutting self off
jokes.explanation: Well-executed immediate proof of claim with perfect timing on the cutoff

jokes.text: The one time your hands aren't sticky, they're greasy! Oh, sorry. Normally I would've wiped them on my pants, but...
jokes.setup: Fry trying to help Leela who's slipping
jokes.punchline: Fry's explanation references the pants that started this whole mess
jokes.explanation: Nice callback tying the pants subplot to current predicament

jokes.text: We made a rope from my shirt and jacket and an expedition flag from my underpants.
jokes.setup: Explaining how they got down to help
jokes.punchline: Increasingly inappropriate use of clothing items culminating in underwear flag
jokes.explanation: Good escalation with absurd visual of underwear as expedition flag

jokes.text: Stop telling me to take your hand. Look, Fry, your noble gestures keep making things worse. Can't you just be a rude, unhelpful jerk like Bender?
jokes.setup: Pattern of Fry's failed attempts to help
jokes.punchline: Leela explicitly asking Fry to be worse person like Bender
jokes.explanation: Great character moment highlighting the irony of good intentions backfiring

jokes.text: When I use up the toilet paper, I don't put on a new roll.
jokes.setup: Asked to demonstrate being a rude, unhelpful jerk
jokes.punchline: Bender's example is hilariously mundane bathroom rudeness
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender moment - his idea of being a jerk is petty household annoyances

jokes.text: What badger could've built this?
jokes.setup: Discovering massive pyramid under city
jokes.punchline: Still fixated on badger as explanation for everything
jokes.explanation: Simple callback but weak - pushing the badger joke too far

jokes.text: Ancient history coming through!
jokes.setup: Careful discussion of fragile, precious artifacts
jokes.punchline: Bender barging through and presumably destroying everything
jokes.explanation: Classic Bender moment destroying careful scholarly work with casual indifference

jokes.text: predating even the Girls of Sumeria.
jokes.setup: Describing how ancient the calendar is
jokes.punchline: Reference to 'Girls Gone Wild' style videos from ancient civilization
jokes.explanation: Decent pop culture reference with historical twist

jokes.text: Sorry. I was eating a can of breakfast and looking at porn.
jokes.setup: Mysterious stew identified on the ancient calendar screen
jokes.punchline: Bender's matter-of-fact admission of disgusting multitasking
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender grossness delivered with characteristic nonchalance

jokes.text: I think it means, 'The sun will erupt, all shall perish,' blah-blah-blah. Get to the point! What does it say about me, Bender?
jokes.setup: Amy translating apocalyptic prophecy
jokes.punchline: Bender immediately making world-ending catastrophe about himself
jokes.explanation: Excellent character moment showing Bender's narcissism even facing apocalypse

jokes.text: The world? That's where I live! Told you it'd say something about me.
jokes.setup: Learning the world will be destroyed
jokes.punchline: Bender's logic that this validates his narcissistic concern
jokes.explanation: Brilliant follow-up showing Bender's twisted logic making everything about him

jokes.text: So, you all done with the computer?
jokes.setup: Dramatic revelation about world ending
jokes.punchline: Mundane request to use the computer after apocalyptic discovery
jokes.explanation: Good comedic timing cutting through dramatic moment with mundane concern

jokes.text: Coward Man away!
jokes.setup: Everyone panicking about the end of the world
jokes.punchline: Hermes giving himself a superhero name for cowardice
jokes.explanation: Excellent economy and character-appropriate self-aware cowardice

jokes.text: Some of us were crazy before it was cool.
jokes.setup: People panicking and acting crazy about the apocalypse
jokes.punchline: Character claiming hipster cred for pre-existing insanity
jokes.explanation: Clever hipster joke applied to insanity during apocalypse

jokes.text: It's not starting. Come ons, comes on!
jokes.setup: Trying to start spaceship during emergency
jokes.punchline: Nixon's frustrated car-starting techniques on spacecraft
jokes.explanation: Typical Nixon frustration with technology, relatable car trouble applied to spaceship

jokes.text: It's like Pat on a Sunday morning.
jokes.setup: Ship won't start during emergency evacuation
jokes.punchline: Nixon's oddly specific comparison to someone named Pat
jokes.explanation: Mysterious reference suggesting Nixon's specific personal experiences

jokes.text: If only I could get back that time I spent watching TRON: Legacy.
jokes.setup: Facing death, reflecting on life regrets
jokes.punchline: Specific regret about watching a mediocre movie
jokes.explanation: Easy pop culture reference joke - functional but not particularly creative

jokes.text: Before we die, I'm gonna find and destroy every remaining copy of TRON: Legacy. It may take a couple of hours, but...
jokes.setup: Leela's regret about watching TRON: Legacy
jokes.punchline: Fry's impossibly ambitious plan to destroy all copies in their final hours
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Fry's misguided romantic gestures extending to media criticism

jokes.text: The Balcony Club? I have an individual membership.
jokes.setup: Someone asking about joining the 'Balcony Club' (implied sex)
jokes.punchline: Zoidberg's innocent misunderstanding about membership type
jokes.explanation: Classic Zoidberg missing sexual innuendo with literal interpretation

jokes.text: Zoidberg, get lost. I am lost. So long.
jokes.setup: Bender telling Zoidberg to get lost
jokes.punchline: Zoidberg's literal interpretation and immediate departure
jokes.explanation: Perfect Zoidberg moment with excellent economy and character consistency

jokes.text: All right, Amy, what's so important that you interrupted my embalming?
jokes.setup: Amy interrupting the Professor during the apocalypse
jokes.punchline: Professor casually mentioning he was embalming himself
jokes.explanation: Good dark humor showing Professor's morbid practicality

jokes.text: It was worth waiting five hours to hear you finish that sentence.
jokes.setup: Amy finally revealing the pyramid is a spaceship
jokes.punchline: Bender commenting on Amy's slow revelation delivery
jokes.explanation: Self-aware joke about dramatic pacing in storytelling

jokes.text: Well, the mausoleum held 10 horny teenagers, so... Maybe 30,000.
jokes.setup: Asked about spaceship capacity
jokes.punchline: Professor's bizarre calculation method using inappropriate reference point
jokes.explanation: Great Professor moment with inappropriate calculation and mysterious backstory

jokes.text: They had their chance. Let's go!
jokes.setup: Discussing moral duty to save as many lives as possible
jokes.punchline: Professor immediately abandoning moral duty for convenience
jokes.explanation: Perfect Professor moment showing his casual cruelty and impatience

jokes.text: It's close, with lots of open space and no Woodward or Bernstein.
jokes.setup: Suggesting Mars as destination
jokes.punchline: Nixon's specific concern about avoiding investigative journalists
jokes.explanation: Excellent Nixon characterization referencing his historical nemeses

jokes.text: Who shall live and who shall die? Step right up to the contrabulous choose-matron!
jokes.setup: Need to choose who gets saved from apocalypse
jokes.punchline: Professor's made-up carnival barker language for life-and-death machine
jokes.explanation: Excellent invented language contrasting carnival frivolity with mortal stakes

jokes.text: Female, crotchety. Needed to keep others in their place. Accepted! Damn straight, kajigger!
jokes.setup: Machine evaluating people for evacuation
jokes.punchline: Machine's brutally honest assessment validated by subject's response
jokes.explanation: Good machine-human interaction with invented profanity 'kajigger'

jokes.text: We just got one of these. Rejected!
jokes.setup: Person expecting to be evaluated for their qualities
jokes.punchline: Machine rejecting them for redundancy like a job application
jokes.explanation: Good concept of apocalypse selection as HR department redundancy

jokes.text: Female, scientist. We were in the same sorority. Accepted! Sigma Beta, see you later! Sigma Beta, see you later!
jokes.setup: Machine supposedly making logical choices for survival
jokes.punchline: Machine accepting Amy based on sorority connections and doing a cheer
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion of logical selection with nepotism and enthusiasm

jokes.text: Male, medical doctor, delicious with butter and lemon. Accepted!
jokes.setup: Machine evaluating Zoidberg for medical skills
jokes.punchline: Machine accepting him for culinary rather than medical value
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about Zoidberg's value as food rather than doctor

jokes.text: Delivery boy, no discernible skills. Accepted! 'Cause I like his pants.
jokes.setup: Machine giving harsh but accurate assessment of Fry
jokes.punchline: Arbitrary acceptance based on the lucky pants that started everything
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback tying the pants subplot to major plot point

jokes.text: Top gun pilot, natural leader, extreme combat training. Rejected! With all spaceships inoperable, we only need one spaceship pilot.
jokes.setup: Machine listing Leela's impressive qualifications
jokes.punchline: Rejecting her for being too qualified in a redundant skill
jokes.explanation: Good irony about qualifications becoming liabilities in specific circumstances

jokes.text: Snake door. Roger.
jokes.setup: Operating ancient alien technology
jokes.punchline: Modern military jargon for incomprehensible alien controls
jokes.explanation: Decent absurdist humor mixing ancient alien tech with military protocol

jokes.text: Male, filthy hand, violent temper. Accepted again!
jokes.setup: Fry trying to get Leela accepted by the machine
jokes.punchline: Machine re-evaluating and accepting him for negative traits
jokes.explanation: Solid character moment showing machine values aggression over kindness

jokes.text: Remember how Bender dumped all that pig's blood on me?
jokes.setup: Leela looking at photo with Fry as they part
jokes.punchline: Romantic moment undercut by reference to disgusting past incident
jokes.explanation: Good callback that undercuts romantic moment with gross memory

jokes.text: I wish I could remember with my boobs.
jokes.setup: Touching moment about remembering Fry in her heart
jokes.punchline: Leela's oddly specific anatomical wish
jokes.explanation: Amusing non-sequitur that breaks romantic tension with anatomical absurdity

jokes.text: Ah-ah-ah. A magician never reveals his secrets. Except the Great Reveal-o. That guy stinks.
jokes.setup: Bender claiming magician's privilege about helping Leela
jokes.punchline: Immediate exception and insult to fictional magician
jokes.explanation: Good structure with Bender's characteristic need to insult someone randomly

jokes.text: I'm a lot of people, but I'm not Fry.
jokes.setup: Leela thanking 'Fry' for noble sacrifice
jokes.punchline: Revealing it was Bender who switched places, not Fry
jokes.explanation: Good plot twist revelation with characteristic Bender phrasing

jokes.text: Admission, $20. Dad! Gleesh. Greetings, Mars men. We come to your planet to construct a gleaming city, a second Earth on which to begin anew. Okay. Admission, $20.
jokes.setup: Dramatic arrival of Earth refugees on Mars
jokes.punchline: Leo Wong's consistent focus on charging admission despite Amy's protest
jokes.explanation: Good character consistency with Leo's capitalism overriding everything

jokes.text: Bender! You're stealing your own stuff! I am? Geez, I better slow down. I'm stealing stuff I don't even need. You want a Torah? Nah, I'm not hungry.
jokes.setup: Fry catching Bender looting during apocalypse
jokes.punchline: Bender's casual attitude about stealing his own things and offering sacred text as food
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender moment with multiple layers of inappropriate behavior

jokes.text: I give you Dickfrancisco!
jokes.setup: Nixon unveiling the new Martian city
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous portmanteau city name combining crude humor with San Francisco
jokes.explanation: Juvenile but functional name joke - cheap humor but fits Nixon's character

jokes.text: The doves didn't magically spring from my hand, but rather were crammed into this netting sewn into my sleeve!
jokes.setup: Magician performing at ceremony
jokes.punchline: Magician immediately revealing his magic trick method
jokes.explanation: Perfect character joke - magician whose defining trait is ruining magic

jokes.text: And now, as a solemn testicle to those left behind on Earth...
jokes.setup: Nixon giving formal speech about monument
jokes.punchline: Malapropism saying 'testicle' instead of 'testament'
jokes.explanation: Classic Nixon malapropism - predictable but character-appropriate

jokes.text: That frightened little girl in the statue reminds me of Fry.
jokes.setup: Looking at heroic monument statue
jokes.punchline: Leela's unflattering comparison of Fry to frightened little girl
jokes.explanation: Good character moment with Leela's blunt assessment of Fry's courage

jokes.text: Hey, Ancient Martian's a very hard language. A... looks just like a...
jokes.setup: Amy defending her mistranslation of the calendar
jokes.punchline: Demonstrating the symbols really do look identical
jokes.explanation: Solid justification for plot mistake with visual comedy element

jokes.text: To the spaceship! There is no spaceship. You had it dismantled to build that statue. To the statue.
jokes.setup: Nixon's dramatic call to escape via spaceship
jokes.punchline: Immediate problem and equally ridiculous backup plan
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation with excellent economy and absurd logic

jokes.text: Oh, what an idiot I was. And by 'I', I mean 'you.'
jokes.setup: Professor seemingly taking responsibility for mistake
jokes.punchline: Immediately deflecting blame to Amy with grammar joke
jokes.explanation: Excellent structure and perfect Professor characterization - seeming self-awareness immediately turned to blame

jokes.text: I should've known better than to trust one of Fry's romantic gestures. Every time he says he loves me, I get killed.
jokes.setup: Leela facing death on Mars
jokes.punchline: Realizing pattern between Fry's love declarations and mortal danger
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark humor about their relationship pattern with deadly consequences

jokes.text: Oh no! The kajigger of Gibraltar!
jokes.setup: Mars approaching Earth and causing destruction
jokes.punchline: Fry's made-up name for the Rock of Gibraltar using earlier nonsense word
jokes.explanation: Decent callback to earlier invented word applied to famous landmark

jokes.text: I have got to quit drinking.
jokes.setup: Witnessing impossible sight of Mars flying past Earth
jokes.punchline: Bender's response suggests he thinks he's hallucinating from alcohol
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Bender assuming impossible reality is alcohol-induced

jokes.text: Or somebody else could do it!
jokes.setup: Fry declaring he'll save Leela again
jokes.punchline: Immediate cowardly backtrack suggesting someone else handle it
jokes.explanation: Perfect Fry moment - good intentions immediately undercut by cowardice

jokes.text: I don't know. Bad things happen when you say that.
jokes.setup: Fry again telling Leela to take his hand
jokes.punchline: Leela recognizing the pattern of disasters following this phrase
jokes.explanation: Good self-aware callback to the running gag of Fry's failed help attempts

jokes.text: No, we're not. Grab your severed arm with your other arm. Ew... Okay.
jokes.setup: Leela's arm torn off, too far apart to reach Fry
jokes.punchline: Scruffy's practical but disgusting solution using detached limb
jokes.explanation: Good gross-out physical comedy with characteristic Scruffy practicality

jokes.text: Don't thank me, thank the ladder.
jokes.setup: Being thanked for rescuing Leela
jokes.punchline: Scruffy deflecting credit to inanimate object
jokes.explanation: Perfect Scruffy moment - humble and matter-of-fact about heroics

jokes.text: I can't be mad. I'm on way too many painkillers.
jokes.setup: Fry asking if she's mad about her arm
jokes.punchline: Medical excuse for emotional state
jokes.explanation: Good practical reason for forgiveness in absurd situation

jokes.text: I mean, you failed, miserably, but you're the only person who loves me enough to try.
jokes.setup: Leela acknowledging Fry's sacrifice attempt
jokes.punchline: Backhanded compliment about his complete failure but genuine love
jokes.explanation: Excellent character moment balancing harsh honesty with genuine appreciation
